title: MazeNavigatorHard — Godot Navigation2D Bootstrap
summary: A medium benchmark task requiring restoration of Godot's hollowed-out 2D navigation subsystem (C++) and implementation of a deterministic headless mini-game (GDScript) that exercises it. Agents pathfind through a maze arena with static walls and dynamic obstacles using RVO avoidance; the game records an event stream and produces a canonical JSON final state verified by SHA-256 determinism across two replay runs.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A Godot engine source tree at /workspace/godot - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (MazeNavigatorHard) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets - Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts
instruction:
You are given:
- A Godot engine source tree at /workspace/godot
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (MazeNavigatorHard)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
- Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts

MazeNavigatorHard spawns agents on a 2D navigation mesh arena with static
and dynamic obstacles. Agents pathfind to waypoints, re-route when the map
changes, and use RVO avoidance to avoid each other. The game records an
event stream and produces a JSON final state with a deterministic SHA-256
hash.

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine repair and verification
1) Complete the intentionally hollowed 2D navigation subsystem implementation.
   Large sections of the runtime query and map-building logic have been removed
   and replaced with empty stubs that compile but produce no useful output.
   The surrounding code that was left intact may also contain subtle correctness
   issues; you should retrieve and cross-reference context across the navigation
   module to ensure end-to-end correctness.
2) Build the Godot editor binary:
     /workspace/scripts/build_godot.sh
3) Run the engine's built-in navigation and RNG test suite:
     /workspace/scripts/run_engine_checks.sh
   All tests must pass with zero failures. Fix any issues you find.

Phase B - Game repair and verification
4) The game scripts under /workspace/game_project/scripts are stubs. Implement them
   so MazeNavigatorHard correctly:
   - Creates a navigation map with static arena walls
   - Spawns agents that pathfind via NavigationServer2D
   - Processes replay events (spawn, target change, dynamic obstacles, closest-point queries)
   - Records a JSONL event stream and produces canonical JSON final state
   The game is roughly 500 lines of GDScript.
5) Run the game twice with the same replay and seed, verify determinism:
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_1 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_2 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
6) Copy the first run's output as the canonical result:
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256 /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
7) Build the determinism report:
     Write /workspace/output/determinism_report.json with fields:
       same_seed_hash_equal, run_1_hash, run_2_hash, run_1_event_lines,
       run_2_event_lines
     (derive hashes from your own replay outputs; no reference SHA256 is shipped in the workspace.)

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/nav_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/nav_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not fabricate hashes; compute them from the actual JSON outputs you generate.
- Do not stub out engine logic; every function must have a correct implementation.
- The navigation query and map builder subsystems must be fully functional,
  not approximated or bypassed.

moduleDag.description: A medium benchmark task requiring restoration of Godot's hollowed-out 2D navigation subsystem (C++) and implementation of a deterministic headless mini-game (GDScript) that exercises it. Agents pathfind through a maze arena with static walls and dynamic obstacles using RVO avoidance; the game records an event stream and produces a canonical JSON final state verified by SHA-256 determinism across two replay runs.

nodes.label: NavMap Builder 2D
nodes.description: Implements the full 6-step navmesh build pipeline: gather_region_polygons (collect all polygon data from active NavigationRegion2D nodes), find_edge_connection_pairs (identify shared edges across region boundaries), merge_edge_connection_pairs (deduplicate and link portal edges), edge_connection_margin_connections (handle nearly-touching edges within the cell margin), navlink_connections (wire NavLink2D teleport portals), and update_map_iteration (commit the finished polygon+connection graph to the NavMapIteration2D snapshot). Without this, the navigation server has no traversable graph and all path queries return empty results.

nodes.label: NavMesh Generator 2D — Baking Pipeline
nodes.description: Implements the navigation mesh baking pipeline: sync (finalize completed baking tasks), parse_source_geometry_data (scene tree geometry collection), bake pipeline (CSG union/difference of traversable vs obstruction polygons using Clipper2, agent radius inflation, carve obstructions, polygon partitioning via polypartition), and async baking via WorkerThreadPool. Without this, runtime baking of navigation polygons from scene geometry is non-functional.

nodes.label: Replay I/O (GDScript)
nodes.description: Implements load_replay (reads a JSON replay definition from disk into a Dictionary), canonicalize_value (deterministic recursive value normalization for stable JSON output), write_canonical_json (sorted-key JSON serialization to disk), and write_events_jsonl (writes one JSON object per line). Also provides event validation, statistics computation, timeline extraction, and event stream merging utilities. These I/O utilities are used by replay_runner to load the input replay file and persist all output files; they have no dependency on the engine navigation subsystem.

nodes.label: Nav Agent 2D — RVO Properties & Callbacks
nodes.description: Implements the RVO agent property synchronization and lifecycle management: set_map (registers/unregisters agent with NavMap2D), is_map_changed (tracks map iteration ID), _update_rvo_agent_properties (writes all RVO2D fields and toggles map controlled-agent registration), dispatch_avoidance_callback (extracts computed velocity from RVO agent, clamps speed, calls user Callable), set_velocity/ set_velocity_forced (syncs preferred/actual velocity to RVO2D::Vector2), set_position/set_max_speed (syncs to RVO fields with avoidance guard), get_avoidance_data (builds Dictionary of all RVO internals for debugging), set_paused (toggles controlled-agent list without full avoidance disable), and update (reads back computed velocity from RVO simulation).

nodes.label: Godot Navigation Server 2D — Core Operations
nodes.description: Implements the server's central lifecycle and dispatch: free_rid (handles freeing maps/regions/links/agents/obstacles with proper cleanup of cross-references), physics_process (flushes command queue, loops active maps calling sync/step/ dispatch_callbacks), get_process_info (returns performance monitor counts for active maps, regions, agents, links, polygons, edges, obstacles), map_get_path (constructs NavMeshQueries2D::NavMeshPathQueryTask2D and invokes query_path), simplify_path (Douglas-Peucker path simplification), get_maps/map_get_links/ map_get_regions/map_get_agents/map_get_obstacles (bulk accessors), internal_free_agent/internal_free_obstacle (paired cleanup), and query_path (task-based path query dispatch).

nodes.label: Nav Region Builder 2D — Build Pipeline
nodes.description: Implements the per-region build steps that prepare polygon data for the map builder: _build_step_process_navmesh_data (parses NavigationPolygon vertices and polygons, computes polygon areas and bounding boxes, builds transformed vertex arrays), _build_step_find_edge_connection_pairs (generates edge keys for all polygon edges, identifies matching pairs across regions using a spatial hash), _build_step_merge_edge_connection_pairs (deduplicates connection pairs, merges overlapping portal edges, populates the final connection list), and build_iteration (orchestrates the three build steps in sequence and marks the region as built).

nodes.label: Nav Map 2D — Sync/Step/Dispatch Cycle
nodes.description: Implements the map's per-frame processing cycle and spatial queries: sync (triggers rebuild of map iteration from dirty regions), step (advances RVO simulation one timestep — computes neighbors, new velocities, updates agent positions), dispatch_callbacks (iterates controlled agents and calls their avoidance callbacks), get_closest_point/get_closest_point_owner/ get_closest_point_info (finds nearest point on navmesh polygons with spatial indexing), get_random_point (uniform random sampling over navmesh surface), set_agent_as_controlled (adds to RVO controlled list), get_region_connections_count/ get_region_connection_pathway_start/end (edge portal accessors), compute_single_avoidance_step (single-agent RVO computation for debug), and the NavMap2D constructor (initializes spatial parameters).

nodes.label: NavMesh Queries 2D (A* Pathfinding)
nodes.description: Implements the complete runtime pathfinding pipeline on top of the built map: emit_callback (safe Callable invocation), polygons_get_random_point (uniform and non-uniform random sampling over polygon surfaces), _query_task_push_back_point_with_metadata (accumulates path waypoints with optional type/RID/owner metadata), _query_task_find_start_end_positions (snaps start/end into the nearest polygon), _query_task_search_polygon_connections (A* relaxation step over polygon adjacency), and map_query_path (top-level entry: runs A* then post-processes via corridor-funnel, edge-centered, or none). Depends on nav_map_builder because it iterates over the NavMapIteration2D polygon graph that the builder populates.

nodes.label: Navigation System Wrapper (GDScript)
nodes.description: Implements the GDScript wrapper around NavigationServer2D: build_arena_navpoly (constructs the outer arena boundary polygon and cuts out static wall obstacles as NavigationPolygon holes), add_navpoly_region (registers it with the active map including transform), get_path (issues a NavigationPathQueryParameters2D request and returns the waypoint PackedVector2Array), get_closest_point (snaps an arbitrary position to the nearest navmesh surface point), update_agent_position, set_agent_preferred_velocity, add_obstacle / remove_obstacle, and force_update. Depends on the engine query pipeline (nav_mesh_queries) and correct agent callbacks (engine_minor_fixes).

nodes.label: Simulation World (GDScript)
nodes.description: Implements the full deterministic simulation: configure (seeds RNG, builds the arena navmesh via nav_system, registers static walls), run (60 Hz tick loop for the replay duration — recomputes paths every PATH_RECOMPUTE_INTERVAL ticks, advances RVO avoidance, checks arrival), _on_agent_avoidance callback (applies safe velocity to agent state), replay-event processing (spawn, target_change, add_obstacle, remove_obstacle, closest_point_query events), and the get_final_state / get_events / get_trace accessors that produce the canonical output. Depends on nav_system_gdscript for all navigation calls.

nodes.label: Replay Runner Entry Point (GDScript)
nodes.description: Minor completion of the headless SceneTree entry-point: adds the require_nav_queries and require_deterministic_rng flags to the world.configure call so that the simulation can enforce those postconditions. Also ensures these flags are parsed from the CLI arguments and forwarded correctly. Depends on sim_world (world.run / get_final_state / get_events / get_trace) and replay_io (load_replay, write_canonical_json, write_events_jsonl).

edges.label: nav_mesh_queries iterates over the NavMapIteration2D polygon+connection graph that nav_map_builder builds; without a built map all A* queries return empty paths
nav_mesh_queries relies on properly baked navigation polygons from the generator to have valid polygon data for pathfinding
nav_system.get_path calls NavigationServer2D.map_query_path which routes to the A* implementation restored in nav_mesh_queries_2d.cpp
sim_world.configure creates the arena navmesh and spawns RVO agents via nav_system; sim_world.run calls nav_system.get_path and force_update every tick
replay_runner instantiates SimWorld, calls world.run(), and reads world.get_final_state() / get_events() / get_trace() for output
replay_runner uses replay_io.load_replay to read the input file and replay_io.write_canonical_json / write_events_jsonl to persist all output files
nav_map_builder consumes region build results produced by nav_region_builder; without built regions the map builder has no polygon data to assemble
nav_mesh_queries depends on the server core's physics_process loop to trigger map sync and flush query tasks before path results are available
nav_system.gd calls NavigationServer2D functions (free_rid, map_force_update, get_process_info) that are implemented in the server core
the server core's physics_process dispatches agent avoidance callbacks that require nav_agent_rvo's _update_rvo_agent_properties and dispatch_avoidance_callback
nav_mesh_queries uses map iteration data (closest point, random point) that is computed during the map's sync/step cycle
nav_region_builder's build steps are triggered during the map iteration sync cycle; without map iteration the region builder is never invoked
